walkthrough.title: Remove 4-Player Cards (2-3 Players Only)
walkthrough.text: Before anything else, check your player count. If you are playing with 2 or 3 players, find the Action cards that have a small number 4 printed in the bottom-right corner. Remove these cards and return them to the box. They are used only in a 4-player game. In a 4-player game, keep all 17 Action cards.

walkthrough.title: Choose Colors and Gather Clans
walkthrough.text: Each player chooses one of the four clan colors. Take all 12 clan figures of your chosen color and place them in front of you. This group of figures is your reserve. Throughout the game, you will recruit clans from your reserve onto the board. If all 12 figures are on the board, you cannot recruit more until some return to your reserve. The different sculpts among your figures are purely cosmetic and have no gameplay effect.

walkthrough.title: Set Out Tokens and Markers
walkthrough.text: Place the following items near the play area where everyone can reach them: the 8 Deed tokens, the 4 Pretender tokens, and the 1 Festival marker. These will be used throughout the game as players earn Deeds, claim Pretender status, and play the Festival Action card.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Epic Tale Deck
walkthrough.text: Find all 30 Epic Tale cards. These are the cards with a distinct colored back representing Celtic legends. Shuffle them thoroughly and place them in a facedown pile near the play area. Players will draw from this deck when certain game effects occur, such as placing a Sanctuary or playing the Bard Action card.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Territory Tile Stack
walkthrough.text: Gather all 16 territory tiles. Shuffle them and place them in a facedown stack near the play area. These tiles represent the island's regions that players will explore and contest throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Create the Starting Map
walkthrough.text: Draw a number of territory tiles from the top of the stack equal to the number of players. For example, draw 3 tiles in a 3-player game. Place these tiles faceup and interlocked at the center of the table. Each tile must be adjacent to at least two other tiles, forming a connected cluster. This is your starting island.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Advantage Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the Advantage cards that match the territory tiles currently on the board. Each territory has a matching Advantage card with the same name. Place these matching Advantage cards faceup near the play area so everyone can see them. Place all remaining Advantage cards in a separate facedown pile. As new territories are added to the island during the game, their matching Advantage cards will be revealed.

walkthrough.title: Assign the Starting Brenn
walkthrough.text: Give the Brenn marker to a randomly chosen player. This player is the temporary leader until a High King is elected. The Brenn organizes the Assembly, acts first during the Season, and has a tiebreaking advantage at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Place the Capital and Sanctuary
walkthrough.text: The Brenn chooses one of the starting territories to be the Capital. Place the larger plastic Capital Citadel in that territory. Then place one Sanctuary in the same territory. The Capital territory is important because its chieftain becomes the Brenn at the start of each Assembly phase.

walkthrough.title: Flip the Flock of Crows Token
walkthrough.text: The Brenn flips the Flock of Crows token like a coin. One side shows clockwise, the other shows counterclockwise. The faceup side determines the direction of turn order for this game. In a 2-player game, the Flock of Crows token is not used except for certain territory effects.

walkthrough.title: Place Starting Clans
walkthrough.text: Starting with the Brenn and proceeding in the direction shown by the Flock of Crows token, each player takes turns placing one clan figure at a time in any territory on the board. Continue around the table until each player has placed exactly two clans on the board. Your two clans can go in the same territory or in different territories. The remaining 10 figures stay in your reserve. You are now ready to play.

summary.title: Remove 4-Player Cards (2-3 Players Only)
bullets: 2-3 players: remove Action cards with a '4' in bottom-right corner
4 players: keep all 17 Action cards

summary.title: Choose Colors and Gather Clans
bullets: Each player takes all 12 clan figures of one color
Place them in front of you as your reserve
Different sculpts are cosmetic only

summary.title: Set Out Tokens and Markers
bullets: Place 8 Deed tokens, 4 Pretender tokens, and 1 Festival marker near the play area
Keep them within reach of all players

summary.title: Prepare the Epic Tale Deck
bullets: Shuffle all 30 Epic Tale cards
Place facedown near the play area

summary.title: Prepare the Territory Tile Stack
bullets: Shuffle all 16 territory tiles
Place facedown in a stack near the play area

summary.title: Create the Starting Map
bullets: Draw territory tiles equal to the number of players (2, 3, or 4)
Place faceup and interlocked so each tile is adjacent to at least 2 others

summary.title: Set Up Advantage Cards
bullets: Find Advantage cards matching starting territories — place faceup near play area
Place remaining Advantage cards in a facedown pile

summary.title: Assign the Starting Brenn
bullets: Give the Brenn marker to a random player
The Brenn leads the Assembly and goes first in the Season

summary.title: Place the Capital and Sanctuary
bullets: The Brenn picks one starting territory as the Capital
Place the Capital Citadel and 1 Sanctuary in that territory

summary.title: Flip the Flock of Crows Token
bullets: Brenn flips the token like a coin
Faceup side determines turn order direction (clockwise or counterclockwise)
Not used in 2-player games (except certain territory effects)

summary.title: Place Starting Clans
bullets: Starting with the Brenn, each player places 1 clan at a time in any territory
Continue in turn order until each player has placed exactly 2 clans
Clans can go in the same or different territories

walkthrough.title: Round Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Inis is played over a series of rounds. Each round has two phases. First is the Assembly Phase, where players check for victory, determine chieftains, set turn order, and draft Action cards. Second is the Season Phase, where players take turns playing cards from their hand to perform actions on the board. Understanding this two-phase rhythm is the key to Inis.

walkthrough.title: Assembly Step 1: Assign Brenn
walkthrough.text: At the start of each Assembly, check who is chieftain of the Capital territory. The chieftain is the player with the most clans there. That player becomes the new Brenn and takes the Brenn marker. If there is a tie for most clans in the Capital, or no one has clans there, the Brenn does not change. The Brenn can only change during this step, never during the Season phase.

walkthrough.title: Assembly Step 2: Check for Victory
walkthrough.text: Now check if anyone has won. Only players who claimed a Pretender token during the previous Season are checked. The three victory conditions are: being present in 6 or more territories, being chieftain over a combined 6 or more opposing clans, or being present in territories with a combined 6 or more Sanctuaries. Deed tokens act as wilds, each adding 1 toward any single condition. The player meeting the most conditions wins. If tied, the Brenn wins among tied players. If the Brenn is not among the tied, no one wins and the game continues. All Pretender tokens are returned to the supply.

walkthrough.title: Assembly Step 3: Take Advantage Cards
walkthrough.text: The chieftain of each territory takes that territory's matching Advantage card into their hand. A chieftain is the player with the most clans in a territory. If there is a tie for most clans, the territory has no chieftain, and the Advantage card stays faceup near the play area. Once a player has an Advantage card, they keep it for the entire round even if they later lose chieftain status.

walkthrough.title: Assembly Step 4: Flip Flock of Crows
walkthrough.text: The Brenn flips the Flock of Crows token like a coin. The faceup side determines the direction of turn order for this round: clockwise or counterclockwise. Any game effect that refers to 'next player' or 'in turn order' follows this direction. In a 2-player game, this step is skipped.

walkthrough.title: Assembly Step 5: Deal Action Cards
walkthrough.text: The Brenn takes all the Action cards, shuffles them, and randomly sets one card aside facedown. In a 4-player game there are 17 Action cards, so after setting one aside, the Brenn deals 4 cards to each player. In a 3-player game there are 13 cards (the 4-player-only cards were removed), so after setting one aside, deal 4 to each player. For a 2-player game, there is a special drafting procedure described separately.

walkthrough.title: Assembly Step 6: Action Card Draft (3-4 Players)
walkthrough.text: The draft has three steps. First, each player looks at their 4 cards, keeps 1, and passes the remaining 3 to the next player in turn order. Second, each player combines the 1 held card with the 3 received cards, looks at all 4, keeps 2, and passes the remaining 2. Third, each player combines the 2 held cards with the 2 received cards, looks at all 4, keeps 3, and passes the remaining 1. After the draft, each player has 4 Action cards, plus any Advantage and Epic Tale cards from earlier. A key detail: you can pass a card you held in a previous step, allowing you to adapt as you see new cards.

walkthrough.title: 2-Player Draft Variant
walkthrough.text: In a 2-player game, the draft works differently. Shuffle all Action cards and set one aside facedown. The Brenn deals 3 cards to each player. Each player keeps 1 and passes 2 to their opponent. Then each player keeps 2 and passes 1. Set these cards aside facedown. Deal 3 more cards to each player and repeat the draft. At the end, each player has 6 Action cards chosen across two separate drafts.

walkthrough.title: Season Phase: Overview
walkthrough.text: The Brenn begins the Season by playing the first card, which must be a Season card. Then each player in turn order chooses one of three options: play a Season card, pass, or take a Pretender token. Play continues around the table until all players have consecutively passed, which ends the Season. Then a new round begins with the Assembly phase.

walkthrough.title: Season Option: Play a Season Card
walkthrough.text: A Season card can be an Action card, an Advantage card, or an Epic Tale card, as long as it has the Season timing icon. Play the card, resolve its effect, then discard it. Action cards go to a facedown discard pile. Advantage cards go facedown near the play area with their back visible. Epic Tale cards go to a faceup discard pile. After playing a card, the next player takes their turn. You do not have to play all your cards. Cards in your hand are always secret, but the number and types visible on their backs are public information.

walkthrough.title: Season Option: Pass
walkthrough.text: You can choose to pass instead of playing a card. You are not eliminated from the round. If the turn comes back to you, you can play a card or pass again. However, if you pass and all other players also consecutively pass after you, the Season ends. Passing is useful for delaying decisions and saving cards, but risky because the Season could end before you act again.

walkthrough.title: Season Option: Take a Pretender Token
walkthrough.text: Instead of playing a card or passing, you can take a Pretender token from the supply. You must currently meet at least one victory condition, and you cannot already have a Pretender token. Place it in front of you. You keep it until the next Assembly phase even if you later stop meeting the conditions. Only players with Pretender tokens are checked for victory during the Assembly. This is the only way to set yourself up to win.

walkthrough.title: Triskel Cards
walkthrough.text: Some cards have a Triskel timing icon instead of or in addition to the Season icon. Triskel cards can only be played during the specific timing described on the card, such as 'when an opponent plays an Action card' or 'during a clash as a maneuver.' Multiple Triskel cards can respond to the same action. The Geis card, which cancels an Action card's effect, can only be played after the current player has fully declared their action. A Triskel card that says 'After you play a Season card' cannot be played until after fully resolving that card's effect, including any clashes.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Action Cards
walkthrough.text: Action cards are the core of the game. They let you recruit clans, move clans, build Citadels and Sanctuaries, explore new territories, and more. You acquire them during the Assembly draft. They are not kept between rounds. If you do not play all your Action cards during the Season, any remaining are discarded at the end of the Season. There are 17 Action cards in total, with 13 used in 2-3 player games.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Advantage Cards
walkthrough.text: Advantage cards are tied to specific territories. The chieftain of each territory takes its matching Advantage card during the Assembly. Once in your hand, you keep it for the entire round even if you lose chieftain status. They are not kept between rounds. When a new territory tile is placed on the board, its matching Advantage card is revealed from the Advantage deck and placed faceup near the play area.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Epic Tale Cards
walkthrough.text: Epic Tale cards represent the Celtic gods and heroes. They are drawn when specific game effects occur, such as placing a Sanctuary or using the Bard Action card. Unlike Action and Advantage cards, Epic Tale cards are kept from round to round until played. Some have Season timing, some have Triskel timing, and some can be used as maneuvers during clashes. There are 30 Epic Tale cards in the deck.

walkthrough.title: Clashes: When They Occur
walkthrough.text: A clash occurs when one or more clans are moved into a territory where opposing clans are already present. Some card effects also cause clashes without movement. Importantly, placing a clan in a territory, such as through the New Clans Action card, does not initiate a clash. Cards that can trigger a clash show a crossed-swords icon in the top-right corner. When a clash begins, the player who moved the clans is the instigator. Resolve the clash immediately before continuing the round.

walkthrough.title: Clashes Step 1: Citadels
walkthrough.text: First, clans can take shelter in the Citadels present in the clashing territory. Starting with the next player after the instigator and proceeding in turn order, each player except the instigator may place one of their clans from that territory into an unoccupied Citadel. This continues in rounds until all Citadels are full or no one else wants to use them. Each Citadel protects exactly one clan. A clan in a Citadel is completely protected and not involved in the clash. The instigator cannot use Citadels.

walkthrough.title: Clashes Step 2: Resolution
walkthrough.text: Starting with the instigator and proceeding in turn order, each player with exposed clans (clans not in Citadels) must perform one maneuver. Before each maneuver, all players with exposed clans may discuss and unanimously agree to end the clash. If they do not agree, the current player must choose one of three maneuvers: Attack, Withdraw, or Epic Tale Maneuver. After each maneuver, check if the clash ends. If no exposed clans remain, the clash ends automatically.

walkthrough.title: Maneuver: Attack
walkthrough.text: Choose an opponent who has exposed clans in the clashing territory. The attacked player then chooses one of two options: discard an Action card from their hand with no effect, or remove one of their exposed clans from the territory and return it to their reserve. If the attacked player has no Action cards in hand, they must remove a clan. There is no way to avoid the consequences of an attack.

walkthrough.title: Maneuver: Withdraw
walkthrough.text: Move one or more of your exposed clans from the clashing territory to adjacent territories where you are the chieftain. The Withdraw maneuver does not initiate another clash. If you have multiple adjacent territories where you are chieftain, you may split your withdrawing clans among them as you wish. If you are not chieftain of any adjacent territory, you cannot choose this maneuver.

walkthrough.title: Maneuver: Epic Tale Maneuver
walkthrough.text: Some Epic Tale cards with the Triskel symbol can be played as a maneuver during a clash. Like all Triskel cards, they can only be played during the specific timing and conditions indicated on the card. For example, Ogma's Eloquence can immediately end a clash when played as a maneuver.

walkthrough.title: End of a Clash
walkthrough.text: A clash ends in one of two ways: all involved players unanimously agree to end it, or no exposed clans remain in the territory. Even if only one player has exposed clans left, they must decide to end the clash or continue performing maneuvers, likely Withdraw. When the clash ends, all clans in Citadels are returned to the territory as normal exposed clans. If the Festival marker is in a clashing territory, the instigator loses one clan before the Citadels step.

walkthrough.title: Chieftain and Territory Rules
walkthrough.text: The player with the most clans in a territory is its chieftain. If there is a tie for most clans, the territory has no chieftain. Being chieftain matters for: receiving the territory's Advantage card during Assembly, becoming the Brenn if you are chieftain of the Capital, having a place to Withdraw clans during clashes, and contributing to the opposing-clans victory condition. A player is present in a territory simply by having one or more clans there.

walkthrough.title: No Clans on the Board
walkthrough.text: If you have no clans in any territory when your turn begins during the Season, you must first discard a Deed token if you have one. Then, whether you discarded a Deed or not, place two clans from your reserve into any territories. After that, take your turn normally: play a Season card, pass, or take a Pretender token.

walkthrough.title: End of the Season
walkthrough.text: When all players have consecutively passed, the Season ends. Players keep all Epic Tale cards in their hands. All remaining Action cards are discarded. If a player has an Advantage card and is no longer chieftain of the matching territory, they place it faceup near the play area. If the Festival marker is in a territory, remove it and place it near the play area. Then begin a new round with the Assembly phase.

walkthrough.title: Victory Conditions and Deeds
walkthrough.text: There are exactly three victory conditions. Territories: be present in 6 or more territories. Chieftain: be chieftain over a combined total of 6 or more opposing clans. Sanctuaries: be present in territories with a combined total of 6 or more Sanctuaries. Each Deed token can add 1 toward any single condition. A Deed used for one condition cannot also count for another. The degree to which you exceed 6 does not matter. Meeting a condition is binary. The player with the most conditions met among Pretender holders wins.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: Victory is checked only during Assembly Step 2. If no player has a Pretender token, no one wins, even if they meet conditions. Among Pretender holders, the one meeting the most victory conditions wins. They must meet at least one. If tied for most conditions: if the Brenn is among the tied players, the Brenn wins. If the Brenn is not among the tied, no one wins and the game continues for another round. All Pretender tokens return to the supply, and the Assembly proceeds.

summary.title: Round Structure Overview
bullets: Each round: Assembly Phase then Season Phase
Assembly: check victory, draft cards, assign chieftains
Season: play cards to perform actions on the board

summary.title: Assembly Step 1: Assign Brenn
bullets: Chieftain of the Capital territory becomes the Brenn
Tie for most clans in Capital = no chieftain, Brenn stays the same
Brenn can only change during this step

summary.title: Assembly Step 2: Check for Victory
bullets: Only Pretender token holders are checked
3 conditions: 6+ territories, 6+ opposing clans as chieftain, 6+ Sanctuaries
Deeds = wilds (+1 to any single condition each)
Most conditions met wins; Brenn breaks ties

summary.title: Assembly Step 3: Take Advantage Cards
bullets: Chieftain of each territory takes its matching Advantage card
Tied territories have no chieftain — card stays on the table
Keep Advantage cards all round even if you lose chieftain status

summary.title: Assembly Step 4: Flip Flock of Crows
bullets: Brenn flips the token like a coin
Determines turn order direction: clockwise or counterclockwise
Skipped in 2-player games

summary.title: Assembly Step 5: Deal Action Cards
bullets: Brenn shuffles all Action cards, sets 1 aside facedown
Deals 4 cards to each player (3-4 player game)
2-player game uses a special double-draft variant

summary.title: Assembly Step 6: Action Card Draft (3-4 Players)
bullets: Round 1: keep 1 card, pass 3
Round 2: keep 2 cards, pass 2
Round 3: keep 3 cards, pass 1
End result: 4 Action cards per player, plus Advantage and Epic Tale cards

summary.title: 2-Player Draft Variant
bullets: Two separate drafts of 3 cards each
Each draft: keep 1 pass 2, then keep 2 pass 1
Total: 6 Action cards per player

summary.title: Season Phase: Overview
bullets: Brenn plays first (must play a Season card)
Each turn: play a Season card, pass, or take a Pretender token
Season ends when all players consecutively pass

summary.title: Season Option: Play a Season Card
bullets: Play any card with a Season timing icon (Action, Advantage, or Epic Tale)
Resolve effect, then discard by type
Action: facedown discard pile. Advantage: facedown near play area. Epic Tale: faceup discard pile

summary.title: Season Option: Pass
bullets: Skip your turn without playing a card
You can still play cards if the turn comes back to you
If all players consecutively pass, the Season ends immediately

summary.title: Season Option: Take a Pretender Token
bullets: Must currently meet at least 1 victory condition
Cannot already have a Pretender token
Token kept until next Assembly, even if conditions no longer met

summary.title: Triskel Cards
bullets: Can only be played at the specific timing on the card
Multiple Triskel cards can respond to the same action
Geis: can only be played after opponent fully declares their action
After-play Triskels: resolve after the card's full effect including clashes

summary.title: Card Types: Action Cards
bullets: Core actions: recruit, move, build, explore
Acquired through Assembly draft, not kept between rounds
17 total (13 in 2-3 player games)

summary.title: Card Types: Advantage Cards
bullets: Tied to specific territories — chieftain takes matching card
Kept for the round even if chieftain status is lost
Not kept between rounds

summary.title: Card Types: Epic Tale Cards
bullets: Drawn via game effects (Sanctuaries, Bard card, etc.)
Kept from round to round until played
30 cards total, inspired by Celtic legends

summary.title: Clashes: When They Occur
bullets: Triggered by moving clans into a territory with opposing clans
Placing clans (e.g. New Clans) does NOT trigger a clash
Resolve immediately before continuing the round
Instigator = the player who moved clans in

summary.title: Clashes Step 1: Citadels
bullets: Non-instigator players may shelter 1 clan per unoccupied Citadel
Proceeds in turn order starting after the instigator
Sheltered clans are fully protected and not involved in the clash
Instigator cannot use Citadels

summary.title: Clashes Step 2: Resolution
bullets: Players with exposed clans take turns performing maneuvers
Before each maneuver, players may unanimously agree to end the clash
Three maneuver options: Attack, Withdraw, Epic Tale Maneuver
Clash ends when no exposed clans remain or all agree to stop

summary.title: Maneuver: Attack
bullets: Target an opponent with exposed clans in the clashing territory
Attacked player chooses: discard an Action card (no effect) OR remove 1 exposed clan
No Action cards in hand = must remove a clan

summary.title: Maneuver: Withdraw
bullets: Move 1+ exposed clans to adjacent territories where you are chieftain
Does not initiate a new clash
Can split clans among multiple valid territories
Cannot Withdraw if you are not chieftain of any adjacent territory

summary.title: Maneuver: Epic Tale Maneuver
bullets: Play an Epic Tale card with Triskel timing as your maneuver
Must meet the timing/conditions on the card
Example: Ogma's Eloquence immediately ends a clash

summary.title: End of a Clash
bullets: Ends when all agree or no exposed clans remain
Citadel clans return to the territory normally
Festival marker in territory: instigator loses 1 clan before Citadels step

summary.title: Chieftain and Territory Rules
bullets: Chieftain = most clans in a territory. Tie = no chieftain
Chieftain gets Advantage card, can become Brenn at Capital
Present = having 1 or more clans in a territory

summary.title: No Clans on the Board
bullets: If you start your turn with 0 clans on the board, discard 1 Deed if able
Then place 2 clans from your reserve in any territories
After that, take your turn as normal

summary.title: End of the Season
bullets: All players consecutively pass = Season over
Keep Epic Tale cards; discard remaining Action cards
Return Advantage cards if no longer chieftain
Remove Festival marker from board if present

summary.title: Victory Conditions and Deeds
bullets: Territories: present in 6+ territories
Chieftain: chieftain over 6+ opposing clans total
Sanctuaries: present where 6+ total Sanctuaries are
Each Deed = +1 toward any single condition (not shared across conditions)

summary.title: Winning the Game
bullets: Victory checked only during Assembly Step 2
Must have a Pretender token to be checked
Most victory conditions met wins (must meet at least 1)
Tie: Brenn wins among tied. Brenn not tied: no winner, play continues

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Island
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with three players: Red, Blue, and Green. Three players is the recommended count for Inis. The board has three starting territories: Valley, Cove, and Plains. The Capital Citadel and one Sanctuary are in the Valley. Red is the Brenn. Each player has placed 2 starting clans. Red has 1 clan in Valley and 1 in Cove. Blue has 2 clans in Plains. Green has 1 clan in Cove and 1 in Plains. Ready? Let us begin the first Assembly.

walkthrough.title: Assembly: Assign Brenn and Check Victory
walkthrough.text: Step 1: Check the Capital territory (Valley). Red has the most clans there with 1 (Blue and Green have 0), so Red remains the Brenn. Step 2: Check for victory. No one has a Pretender token yet, so there is no winner to check. This is the first round, so we move on.

walkthrough.title: Assembly: Advantage Cards and Turn Order
walkthrough.text: Step 3: Determine chieftains. Red is chieftain of Valley (1 clan, no competition) and chieftain of Cove (1 clan, tied with Green's 1 clan — wait, that is a tie, so Cove has no chieftain). Blue is chieftain of Plains (2 clans vs. Green's 1). Green is chieftain of nowhere alone. Red takes the Valley Advantage card. Blue takes the Plains Advantage card. The Cove Advantage card stays on the table. Step 4: Red flips the Flock of Crows token. It lands on clockwise.

walkthrough.title: Assembly: Draft Action Cards
walkthrough.text: Step 5: Red shuffles all 13 Action cards (3-player game), sets 1 aside facedown, and deals 4 to each player. Step 6: The draft begins. Each player examines their 4 cards, keeps 1, and passes 3 clockwise. Then keeps 2, passes 2. Then keeps 3, passes 1. After drafting, each player has 4 Action cards plus any Advantage and Epic Tale cards. The Assembly is complete. Time for the Season.

walkthrough.title: Red's First Turn: New Clans
walkthrough.text: The Season begins. Red is the Brenn, so Red goes first and must play a Season card. Red plays the New Clans Action card, which allows placing clans in territories where Red is present. Red places 1 new clan in Valley and 1 new clan in Cove from the reserve. Remember, placing clans does not trigger a clash. Red now has 2 clans in Valley, 2 clans in Cove.

walkthrough.title: Blue's First Turn: Exploration
walkthrough.text: Blue plays the Exploration Action card. Blue draws the top territory tile from the stack. It is the Forest tile. Blue places it adjacent to at least two existing territories, connecting it to Plains and Valley. Blue then places one clan from the reserve in the new Forest territory. The matching Forest Advantage card is taken from the Advantage deck and placed faceup near the play area. Blue now has a foothold in a new region.

walkthrough.title: Green's First Turn: The Mistake — Reckless Migration
walkthrough.text: Green plays the Migration Action card, which allows moving clans from one territory to one or more other territories. Green moves both clans: the one from Cove and the one from Plains, sending both to Valley where Red has 2 clans. This triggers a clash! Green moved into a territory with opposing clans. Green is the instigator. But here is the problem: Valley has the Capital Citadel. Red can shelter one clan, leaving Red with 1 exposed and Green with 2 exposed. Green is outnumbered in a territory with a Citadel they cannot use because they are the instigator.

walkthrough.title: Resolving the Clash
walkthrough.text: Citadels step: Red has 2 clans in Valley. Starting with the next player after Green (the instigator), Red can shelter one clan in the Capital Citadel. Red does so. Now Red has 1 exposed clan, Green has 2 exposed clans. Resolution step: Green is the instigator and goes first. Green chooses Attack and targets Red. Red must discard an Action card or remove a clan. Red discards an Action card. Now it is Red's turn. Red attacks Green. Green has to discard an Action card or lose a clan. Green discards an Action card. Green attacks Red again. Red removes a clan. The clash continues until they negotiate an end or clans are removed.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Don't Rush Into Citadel Territories
walkthrough.text: Green made a classic mistake. By moving into a territory with a Citadel as the instigator, Green could not protect any clans. Meanwhile, Red sheltered one safely. Green also abandoned two territories, losing presence across the board. In Inis, aggressive movement is sometimes necessary, but attacking a fortified position when you are the instigator puts you at a serious disadvantage. Always count the Citadels before you charge in.

walkthrough.title: Red's Second Turn: Sanctuary
walkthrough.text: After the clash resolves, play continues. Red plays the Druid Action card (or another available card) and later plays a card that allows building a Sanctuary. Red places a Sanctuary in Cove where Red has clans. Whenever a Sanctuary is placed, the player draws 1 Epic Tale card. Red draws from the Epic Tale deck. Sanctuaries are crucial for the Sanctuary victory condition and also grant access to the powerful Epic Tale cards.

walkthrough.title: Blue's Second Turn: Craftsmen & Peasants
walkthrough.text: Blue plays the Craftsmen and Peasants Action card. In each territory where Blue is present, Blue may place 1 clan for each Citadel in that territory. Blue has a clan in the Forest (no Citadels there) and in Plains (no Citadels). Blue gains nothing from this card! Wait — Blue could have drafted a more useful card if Blue had paid attention to the board state. Blue wasted a card play.

walkthrough.title: Green's Mistake: Forgetting About the Pretender Token
walkthrough.text: Green now has clans spread thin after the failed migration. Green meets zero victory conditions: only present in 1 or 2 territories, not chieftain over 6 opposing clans, and not near enough Sanctuaries. Green cannot take a Pretender token because Green does not meet any conditions. But more importantly, Green has been so focused on fighting that Green forgot the real goal: you need to spread across territories and build toward conditions, not just attack. Clashing costs cards and clans, and Green has spent resources without making progress toward victory.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Clashes Are Costly — Play for Position
walkthrough.text: The takeaway from Green's round: in Inis, military aggression is a tool, not a strategy. Every clash costs Action cards and clans. Green lost cards that could have been used to explore, recruit, or build Sanctuaries. The three victory conditions reward territory presence, Sanctuary presence, and chieftain status — all of which require building up, not tearing down. Use clashes strategically to deny an opponent a victory condition, not as your primary plan.

walkthrough.title: Continue the Season
walkthrough.text: Play continues in clockwise order. Each player plays Season cards, passes, or takes Pretender tokens. Red builds board presence steadily. Blue expands into new territories through exploration. Green recovers by recruiting new clans and spreading out. When all players consecutively pass, the Season ends. All Action cards are discarded. Advantage cards return to the table if the player is no longer chieftain. Epic Tale cards are kept.

walkthrough.title: End of Round 1
walkthrough.text: The Season has ended. A new Assembly phase begins. The Brenn is reassigned based on who is chieftain of the Capital. Victory is checked, but only for players with Pretender tokens. In this first round, if no one took a Pretender token, the game simply continues. New Action cards are dealt, a new draft occurs, and the next Season begins. The island grows as territories are explored, Sanctuaries are built, and clans spread across the land.

walkthrough.title: Green's Mistake: Ignoring the Draft
walkthrough.text: Looking back at the round, Green's biggest mistake actually happened during the draft. Green drafted cards focused on movement and combat, ignoring cards like Exploration and Bard that would help expand presence and draw Epic Tale cards. In Inis, the draft IS the game. The cards you choose determine everything you can do. If you draft only aggressive cards, you can only be aggressive. Draft a balanced hand with options for building, exploring, and recruiting, not just fighting.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen a full round of Inis, including the Assembly draft, Season card play, a clash with Citadels, and three common mistakes: rushing into a Citadel territory as the instigator, playing too aggressively at the cost of victory condition progress, and drafting an imbalanced hand. You now understand the flow of the game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Conquer
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First — check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second — reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every card type, game term, and special rule.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. May your clans find glory on the island!

summary.title: Welcome to the Island
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 3 players: Red (Brenn), Blue, and Green
3 starting territories: Valley (Capital + Sanctuary), Cove, Plains
Each player starts with 2 clans on the board

summary.title: Assembly: Assign Brenn and Check Victory
bullets: Red is chieftain of Valley, so Red stays Brenn
No Pretender tokens yet — skip victory check
First round always proceeds to drafting

summary.title: Assembly: Advantage Cards and Turn Order
bullets: Red takes Valley's Advantage card (chieftain there)
Blue takes Plains' Advantage card (chieftain there)
Cove has tied clans = no chieftain, card stays on table
Flock of Crows flipped: clockwise turn order

summary.title: Assembly: Draft Action Cards
bullets: 13 Action cards (3-player game), 1 set aside, 4 dealt to each
Draft: keep 1 pass 3, keep 2 pass 2, keep 3 pass 1
Each player ends with 4 Action cards plus Advantage/Epic Tale cards

summary.title: Red's First Turn: New Clans
bullets: Red plays New Clans, places clans in Valley and Cove
Placing clans does NOT trigger a clash
Red now has stronger presence in two territories

summary.title: Blue's First Turn: Exploration
bullets: Blue draws a new territory tile (Forest) and places it on the map
Blue places 1 clan in the new territory
Forest's matching Advantage card is revealed

summary.title: Green's First Turn: The Mistake — Reckless Migration
bullets: Green moves both clans into Valley, triggering a clash
Valley has a Citadel — Red can shelter, Green cannot (instigator)
Green abandoned other territories and is outnumbered in the clash

summary.title: Resolving the Clash
bullets: Citadels step: Red shelters 1 clan, Green cannot (instigator)
Resolution: players alternate Attack maneuvers, discarding cards or removing clans
Clashes cost both cards and clans for all involved

summary.title: Lesson: Don't Rush Into Citadel Territories
bullets: Instigator cannot use Citadels — huge disadvantage
Count Citadels before initiating a clash
Green lost positioning and wasted cards for little gain

summary.title: Red's Second Turn: Sanctuary
bullets: Red places a Sanctuary and draws 1 Epic Tale card
Sanctuaries count toward the Sanctuary victory condition
Epic Tale cards are powerful and kept between rounds

summary.title: Blue's Second Turn: Craftsmen & Peasants
bullets: Blue plays Craftsmen & Peasants but gains nothing (no Citadels in Blue's territories)
Wasted a card play — should have checked board state first
Always read cards in context of the current board

summary.title: Green's Mistake: Forgetting About the Pretender Token
bullets: Green meets 0 victory conditions after aggressive play
Cannot take a Pretender token without meeting a condition
Fighting without building toward conditions wastes resources

summary.title: Lesson: Clashes Are Costly — Play for Position
bullets: Clashes cost Action cards and clans — both are precious
Victory conditions reward building up, not tearing down
Use clashes to deny opponent victory conditions, not as a primary strategy

summary.title: Continue the Season
bullets: Players continue taking turns until all consecutively pass
Red builds presence, Blue explores, Green recovers
Season ends: discard Action cards, return unclaimed Advantage cards, keep Epic Tales

summary.title: End of Round 1
bullets: New Assembly begins: reassign Brenn, check victory, draft new cards
No Pretender tokens = no winner check needed
Island grows each round as territories are explored

summary.title: Green's Mistake: Ignoring the Draft
bullets: Green drafted only aggressive cards, missing Exploration and Bard
The draft determines everything you can do during the Season
Draft a balanced hand: building, exploring, recruiting, and some combat

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You have seen a full round: Assembly draft, Season play, clash resolution
Three mistakes: attacking Citadel territory as instigator, over-aggression, bad drafting
You understand the flow of Inis — ready to play!

summary.title: Before You Conquer
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics for beginners
Reference the Appendix for quick-reference on cards and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide — tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: The Draft Is the Game
walkthrough.text: In Inis, the only way to do anything is by playing cards. Every action on the board — recruiting, moving, building, exploring — requires a card. This means the Assembly draft is the most important phase of each round. Pay attention to what cards you are passing, not just what you are keeping. If you see an opponent's Advantage card showing they control a key territory, consider hate-drafting a card that would help them. Always draft at least one card that lets you recruit or place clans, and at least one that lets you expand or build.

walkthrough.title: Spread Early, Consolidate Later
walkthrough.text: New players often cluster their clans in one or two territories trying to dominate them. This is a trap. The presence victory condition requires being in 6 territories, and you only start with 2 clans. Use Exploration and movement cards to spread across the board in the early rounds. Having even one clan in a territory gives you presence there, contributes to the Sanctuary condition if that territory has one, and makes you harder to eliminate. In later rounds, consolidate your positions where it matters most.

walkthrough.title: Sanctuaries Are the Hidden Engine
walkthrough.text: Building Sanctuaries does three things. First, they count toward the Sanctuary victory condition — be present in territories with 6 or more total Sanctuaries. Second, every time you place a Sanctuary, you draw an Epic Tale card, which are the most powerful cards in the game and carry over between rounds. Third, Sanctuaries benefit anyone present in the territory, so you can gain from Sanctuaries other players built. Prioritize the Bard and New Sanctuary Action cards during drafting.

walkthrough.title: Understand Chieftain Control
walkthrough.text: Being chieftain of a territory gives you its Advantage card and counts opposing clans toward your chieftain victory condition. But chieftain status is fragile — it changes whenever someone adds or removes clans. Ties mean no chieftain at all. Do not over-invest in one territory to maintain chieftain status. Instead, maintain slim majorities across several territories. Having 3 clans where an opponent has 2 is enough. Having 6 clans where no one else is present gives you chieftain but wastes clans that could be elsewhere.

walkthrough.title: Pass Strategically
walkthrough.text: Passing during the Season is not a sign of weakness. Every time you pass, your opponents must decide whether to play cards or pass themselves. If they play a card, you get another chance to act with full information. If everyone passes, the Season ends and you keep your Epic Tale cards for next round. Experienced players often pass early to force opponents to reveal their plans. The key is that passing does not lock you out — you can play a card on your next turn if someone else acts after your pass.

walkthrough.title: Don't Fight Without Purpose
walkthrough.text: Clashes are expensive. Each attack either costs the defender an Action card or a clan. Withdrawing requires chieftain status in adjacent territories. The instigator cannot use Citadels. Before moving clans into an opponent's territory, ask yourself: what do I gain from this clash? If the answer is denying a key victory condition or taking chieftain status of a critical territory, go ahead. If you are just fighting because you can, save your cards and clans for building toward your own conditions.

walkthrough.title: Watch for Pretender Timing
walkthrough.text: You can only win if you have a Pretender token, and you can only take one when you meet at least one victory condition. Many new players forget to take their Pretender token or wait too long thinking they need to meet multiple conditions first. You only need one condition to take the token. Once you have it, you are checked for victory at the next Assembly. Meanwhile, opponents will try to knock you below the threshold. Take your Pretender token as soon as you qualify, then defend your position.

walkthrough.title: Use Deeds Wisely
walkthrough.text: Deed tokens are wild bonuses that each add 1 toward any single victory condition. They are earned through specific cards like the Bard and Master Craftsman. Each Deed can only count toward one condition, so plan which condition you are supplementing. Two Deeds can turn a player with 4 Sanctuary-adjacent territories into one meeting the Sanctuary condition at 6. Deeds are also lost if you have zero clans on the board and must re-enter. Protect your Deed-earning cards and track how many Deeds opponents have.

summary.title: The Draft Is the Game
bullets: Every action requires a card — the draft determines your entire round
Draft a balanced hand: recruiting, building, exploring, and movement
Consider hate-drafting cards that would help opponents
Watch what you pass — you are fueling your neighbors

summary.title: Spread Early, Consolidate Later
bullets: Presence in 6 territories is a victory condition — spread out early
Even 1 clan gives you presence and Sanctuary access
Don't cluster all clans in one territory
Consolidate in later rounds once you have broad presence

summary.title: Sanctuaries Are the Hidden Engine
bullets: Count toward the Sanctuary victory condition (6+ total)
Drawing an Epic Tale card each time you place one
Epic Tales are the most powerful cards and carry over between rounds
Prioritize Bard and Sanctuary-building cards in the draft

summary.title: Understand Chieftain Control
bullets: Chieftain = most clans in a territory (gives Advantage card)
Ties = no chieftain (territory's card stays unclaimed)
Maintain slim majorities (3 vs 2) rather than massive stacks
Spread clans for more chieftainships, not fewer with more clans each

summary.title: Pass Strategically
bullets: Passing does not lock you out — you can play next turn if someone else acts
Forces opponents to reveal plans before you commit
If all pass, Season ends and you keep Epic Tale cards
Use passing to save powerful cards for the right moment

summary.title: Don't Fight Without Purpose
bullets: Clashes cost Action cards and clans — both are limited
Instigator cannot use Citadels during the clash
Only clash to deny a victory condition or gain a critical chieftainship
Pointless aggression wastes resources with no strategic benefit

summary.title: Watch for Pretender Timing
bullets: Must have a Pretender token to be checked for victory
Take one as soon as you meet any single condition
You keep it even if conditions change before the next Assembly
Be ready to defend your position once you reveal yourself as a Pretender

summary.title: Use Deeds Wisely
bullets: Each Deed = +1 toward any one victory condition (wild bonus)
Earned via Bard, Master Craftsman, and other card effects
Plan which condition each Deed will supplement
Lost if you re-enter the board with 0 clans — protect your position

walkthrough.title: The Expansion Victory Path
walkthrough.text: The territories victory condition requires presence in 6 or more territories. This is often the fastest path to a Pretender token because you only need one clan in each territory. Use Exploration to add new tiles and immediately place a clan there. Use Migration and movement cards to spread single clans across the board. The key resource is Action cards that let you explore and move, such as Exploration, Migration, and the Manannan's Horses Epic Tale card. Draft these aggressively. The weakness of this path is vulnerability: single clans are easily displaced by opponents.

walkthrough.title: The Chieftain Dominance Path
walkthrough.text: The chieftain victory condition requires being chieftain over 6 or more opposing clans total. This means you need majorities in territories that also contain many opposing clans. The ideal scenario is being chieftain in 2-3 territories where opponents each have 2-3 clans. Cards like New Clans and Craftsmen and Peasants are critical for placing large numbers of clans. The Citadel presence in your territories helps defend your majorities during clashes. This path is strong because it is hard for opponents to disrupt without losing their own clans.

walkthrough.title: The Sanctuary Path
walkthrough.text: The Sanctuary victory condition requires being present in territories with 6 total Sanctuaries. You do not need to build them yourself — just be present where they are. Use the Bard Action card to draw Epic Tale cards (and earn Deeds), and cards that allow building Sanctuaries. Each Sanctuary built earns an Epic Tale card, creating a compound advantage. Build Sanctuaries in territories where you have strong presence and opponents would struggle to push you out. With 10 total Sanctuaries in the game, controlling access to Sanctuary-heavy territories is a strong endgame position.

walkthrough.title: Multi-Condition Victory
walkthrough.text: The real power play in Inis is meeting multiple victory conditions simultaneously. A player who meets two conditions almost always wins, since opponents rarely meet more than one. Deed tokens are the bridge — each one adds 1 toward a condition of your choice. If you have 5 territories and 5 Sanctuaries, two Deeds let you claim both conditions. Build toward two conditions that share the same board positions. Spread across Sanctuary-heavy territories to work toward both the territory and Sanctuary conditions at once.

walkthrough.title: Controlling the Capital and Brenn
walkthrough.text: The Brenn has enormous advantages: they go first in the Season, organize the Assembly, and break ties for victory. Being Brenn means that in a tied game you simply win. To control the Brenn, maintain chieftain status in the Capital territory. The Capital also starts with a Citadel, making it easier to defend. Experienced players often contest the Capital heavily in the mid-game. If you are Brenn, you can time your Pretender token knowing that any tie favors you. This creates a win condition that opponents cannot easily prevent.

walkthrough.title: Clash Timing and Denial
walkthrough.text: The best time to initiate a clash is when your opponent is about to meet a victory condition. If an opponent has clans in 5 territories and is about to explore a 6th, clashing in one of their weak territories to remove their presence is a critical denial play. The attacking maneuver forces the defender to either discard an Action card or lose a clan. Both are costly. Time your clashes for maximum impact: when the opponent has few Action cards left (they cannot absorb attacks) or when removing one clan eliminates their chieftainship or territory presence.

walkthrough.title: Epic Tale Card Management
walkthrough.text: Epic Tale cards are the strongest cards in the game because they carry over between rounds and have unique, powerful effects. Dagda's Cauldron can bring removed clans back. The Morrigan lets you remove an enemy clan from any territory. Ogma's Eloquence ends a clash immediately. Lug's Spear guarantees victory in an attack. Prioritize earning these by building Sanctuaries and drafting the Bard card. Keep track of which Epic Tales opponents hold (the card backs reveal the type). A well-timed Epic Tale card can completely change the game state.

walkthrough.title: Advanced Drafting: Reading the Table
walkthrough.text: During the draft, pay attention to what cards you receive from your neighbor. If they passed you the Exploration card, they likely do not need more territories. If they kept the New Clans card, they are building up in existing territories. Use this information to predict opponent strategies and draft accordingly. Also, consider passing strong cards away from the player who benefits most. If the player to your left is close to a Sanctuary victory, do not pass them the Bard card. Late in the draft (step 3), you know exactly what your neighbor will receive because you are passing them a single card. Use this to control their options.

walkthrough.title: The Geis and Defensive Play
walkthrough.text: The Geis Epic Tale card is one of the most powerful defensive tools in the game. It cancels any Action card effect immediately. Holding Geis in your hand creates a threat even if you never play it — opponents must account for the possibility that their key card will be neutralized. This is especially powerful in the late game when a single Action card could let an opponent meet a victory condition. Combine Geis with strategic passing: save it as your last card, and opponents will be afraid to commit their plans while you still have cards in hand.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Timing and the Pretender Race
walkthrough.text: The endgame of Inis is a delicate dance. You want to be the first to take a Pretender token, but you also want to meet the most conditions when victory is checked. If you take a Pretender token early, opponents can spend the rest of the Season knocking you off conditions. If you wait too long, the Season might end before you can take one. The ideal timing is to take your Pretender token near the end of the Season when opponents have spent their Action cards. Build toward conditions quietly, then claim your Pretender when opponents are out of resources to stop you. Remember, all Pretender tokens reset each Assembly, so this race happens every round.

summary.title: The Expansion Victory Path
bullets: Presence in 6+ territories — only need 1 clan in each
Key cards: Exploration, Migration, Manannan's Horses
Fastest path to Pretender token but vulnerable to single-clan removal
Draft movement and exploration cards aggressively

summary.title: The Chieftain Dominance Path
bullets: Chieftain over 6+ opposing clans — need majorities in crowded territories
Key cards: New Clans, Craftsmen & Peasants
Citadels help defend your majorities during clashes
Hard for opponents to disrupt without losing their own clans

summary.title: The Sanctuary Path
bullets: Present in territories with 6+ total Sanctuaries
Don't need to build them — just be present where they are
Building Sanctuaries draws Epic Tale cards (compound advantage)
10 total Sanctuaries in the game — control access to Sanctuary-heavy territories

summary.title: Multi-Condition Victory
bullets: Meeting 2 conditions almost guarantees a win
Deeds bridge the gap (e.g. 5 territories + 1 Deed = 6)
Build toward conditions that share board positions
Spread across Sanctuary-heavy territories for territory + Sanctuary synergy

summary.title: Controlling the Capital and Brenn
bullets: Brenn breaks ties for victory — huge endgame advantage
Capital starts with a Citadel, easier to defend
Maintain chieftain status in the Capital territory
As Brenn, any tied victory check favors you

summary.title: Clash Timing and Denial
bullets: Best time to clash: when opponent is one step from a victory condition
Attack when opponent has few Action cards (cannot absorb hits)
Removing 1 clan can eliminate a chieftainship or territory presence
Use clashes as surgical denial, not as general strategy

summary.title: Epic Tale Card Management
bullets: Strongest cards in the game — carry over between rounds
Key cards: Dagda's Cauldron (revive), The Morrigan (snipe), Geis (cancel), Ogma's Eloquence (end clash)
Build Sanctuaries and draft Bard to maximize Epic Tale draws
Track which Epic Tales opponents hold by card backs

summary.title: Advanced Drafting: Reading the Table
bullets: Received cards reveal what your neighbor doesn't need
Kept cards reveal opponent strategy (New Clans = building up, Exploration = spreading)
Hate-draft cards away from the player who benefits most
In step 3 you pass exactly 1 card — use it to control their options

summary.title: The Geis and Defensive Play
bullets: Geis cancels any Action card effect — strongest defensive tool
Holding it creates a threat even if unplayed
Save it for late-game when a single card could decide victory
Combine with passing: opponents fear committing while you hold cards

summary.title: Endgame Timing and the Pretender Race
bullets: Take Pretender token near end of Season when opponents are low on cards
Early claim = more time for opponents to disrupt you
Build toward conditions quietly, then strike when opponents are spent
All Pretender tokens reset each Assembly — this race repeats every round

entries.definition: One figure of your color. Each player has 12 clan figures. Clans on the board represent your presence in territories. Clans not on the board are in your reserve and can be recruited.
The player with the most clans in a territory. In case of a tie, the territory has no chieftain. The chieftain receives that territory's Advantage card during the Assembly phase.

A temporary leader chosen to guide the tribes. Determined at random at start of game, then assigned to the Capital's chieftain each Assembly. The Brenn organizes the Assembly, acts first during the Season, and breaks ties for victory.
The territory chosen by the Brenn at the start of the game. Contains the larger Capital Citadel. The chieftain of the Capital becomes the Brenn at each Assembly.
A player is present in a territory if they have one or more clans there, even if they are not the chieftain.
The pool of clan figures a player has not yet placed on the board. Clans are recruited from the reserve. Removed clans return to the reserve for reuse.
The player who initiated a clash, typically by moving clans into a territory with opposing clans. The instigator cannot use Citadels during the clash and goes first in the resolution step.
During a clash, any clan in the clashing territory that is not sheltered in a Citadel. Exposed clans must perform maneuvers and can be targeted by attacks.
An action performed by a player with exposed clans during a clash. The three options are Attack, Withdraw, and Epic Tale Maneuver. Passing is not allowed during a clash.
A clash maneuver. Choose an opponent with exposed clans. They must either discard an Action card (no effect) or remove one exposed clan to their reserve.

A clash maneuver. Move one or more of your exposed clans to adjacent territories where you are chieftain. Does not initiate a new clash. Cannot be chosen if you are not chieftain of any adjacent territory.
A clash maneuver. Play an Epic Tale card with Triskel timing as your maneuver, following the card's specific timing and conditions.

A fortified building placed in a territory. During clashes, non-instigator players can shelter one clan per Citadel. There are 10 Citadels in the game, including the larger Capital Citadel. There is no limit to how many buildings a territory can contain.
The larger Citadel placed in the Capital territory during setup. Functions exactly like other Citadels — protects one clan during a clash.
A place of worship. Being present in territories with 6 or more total Sanctuaries is a victory condition. Placing a new Sanctuary draws 1 Epic Tale card. There are 10 Sanctuaries in the game.
One of 16 tiles representing island regions. New territories are always placed adjacent to at least 2 existing territories. When placed, its matching Advantage card is revealed.
A marker given to the current Brenn player. Transfers to the Capital's chieftain during each Assembly phase.

Flipped like a coin by the Brenn during each Assembly. One side shows clockwise, the other counterclockwise. Determines turn order direction for the round. Not used in 2-player games except for certain territory effects.

Taken during the Season when a player meets at least one victory condition. Only players with Pretender tokens are checked for victory during the Assembly. Kept until the next Assembly even if conditions are no longer met. All returned to supply after victory check.

A wild bonus earned through card effects like the Bard and Master Craftsman. Each Deed adds +1 toward any single victory condition. Each Deed can only be applied to one condition. Lost if you re-enter the board with 0 clans.
Placed in a territory when the Festival Action card is played. If a clash occurs in that territory, the instigator loses one clan before the Citadels step. Removed at end of the Season.

The primary card type, acquired during the Assembly draft. Allows actions like recruiting clans, building, exploring, and moving. Not kept between rounds — unused Action cards are discarded when the Season ends. 17 total (13 in 2-3 player games).

Tied to specific territories. Chieftain of each territory takes its matching Advantage card during the Assembly. Kept for the entire round even if chieftain status is lost. Not kept between rounds. 16 total (1 per territory tile).

Powerful cards representing Celtic legends. Drawn through game effects like placing Sanctuaries or the Bard card. Kept from round to round until played. Some have Season timing, some have Triskel timing, some can be used as clash maneuvers. 30 total.
A timing icon on a card (shown in top-left corner). Season cards are played during the Season phase on your turn as your action. Most Action, Advantage, and some Epic Tale cards have this timing.

A timing icon on a card indicating it can only be played at a specific moment described on the card, such as during a clash or in response to an opponent's action. Multiple Triskel cards can respond to the same trigger.
Action card. Place new clans from your reserve into territories where you are present. Placing clans does not initiate a clash.
Action card. Draw a territory tile, place it adjacent to at least 2 existing territories, and place 1 clan from your reserve in the new territory. The new territory's Advantage card is revealed.

Action card. Move clans from one territory to one or more other territories. May initiate clashes in destination territories with opposing clans. If multiple clashes result, the instigator chooses the order of resolution.
Action card. Draw 1 Epic Tale card. If one of your maneuvers during a clash removes 1 or more opposing clans, gain 1 Deed token.
Action card. In each territory where you are present, place 1 clan from your reserve for each Citadel in that territory. Does not initiate clashes.
Action card. Place the Festival marker in a territory. If a clash occurs there, the instigator loses 1 clan before the Citadels step. Marker is removed at end of Season.

Action card. Has both Season and Triskel timing. Season effect: discard 1 card if able, then draw 1 Epic Tale card. Triskel effect: after you play an Epic Tale card, instead of discarding it, give it to any other player and gain 1 Deed.
Action card. Allows looking at the facedown Action card discard pile. Provides information about which cards opponents have played.
Action card. Look at the Action cards in 1 opponent's hand. Then you may move 1 or more of your clans from 1 territory to 1 adjacent territory. 4-player only card.
Epic Tale card (Triskel timing). When an opponent plays an Action card, ignore the effect of that Action card and discard it. One of the most powerful defensive cards in the game.
Epic Tale card. Can bring removed clans back from the reserve, restoring board presence.
Epic Tale card. Remove 1 clan from any territory. A powerful targeted removal that can disrupt chieftain status or eliminate presence.
Epic Tale card. Can be played as a maneuver during a clash to immediately end the clash. Extremely powerful for stopping unfavorable conflicts.
Epic Tale card. Ensures victory for its possessor in combat situations.
Epic Tale card. The Dagda's weapon has the power to both kill and restore life.
Epic Tale card. A divine harp that plays songs of sadness, laughter, and sleep, giving rhythm to nature and the four seasons.
Epic Tale card. During a clash, at the end of the Citadels step: take all clans out of their Citadels in this territory. They become exposed clans.
Epic Tale card. Remove 1 clan from any territory. Represents the evil eye of Balor, king of the Fomorians.
Epic Tale card. Allows movement of clans across territories, representing the magical horses created from sea foam by Manannan mac Lir.
Epic Tale card. Represents the Fir Bolg champion's determination. Even on the edge of defeat, provides resilience in clashes.
Epic Tale card. Based on the druid Tuan who survived centuries by transforming into different animals. He is History's memory.
Epic Tale card. Based on Deirdre, whose beauty caused many tragedies.
Epic Tale card. Based on one of the greatest Celtic heroes, son of the god Lug, who defended Ulster single-handedly.
Epic Tale card. Based on the story of Grainne's elopement with Diarmuid, pursued endlessly by Fionn mac Cumhaill.
Epic Tale card. The goddess who personifies Ireland. The Tuatha De Danann promised to name the island after her.
Epic Tale card. Tir na nOg, the land of gods, dead people, and endless joy — the realm of eternal youth.
Epic Tale card. The devastating second battle between the Tuatha De Danann and the Fomorians, during which Lugh killed Balor.
Epic Tale card. Based on Cathbad, a druid, prophet, and warrior who was the father of King Conchobar.
Epic Tale card. The best part of a banquet, reserved for the most prestigious warrior. Could lead to many arguments.
Epic Tale card. Brought to Ireland by the Tuatha De Danann, this stone roars when touched by a man worthy to rule.
Epic Tale card. Oengus took possession of his father's castle by asking to live there 'day and night,' meaning for eternity.
Epic Tale card. Tailtu opened the forest for cultivation before dying of exhaustion. Her adoptive son Lug established the harvest festival Lughnasadh in her honor.
Epic Tale card. Breas 'the Beautiful' became a tyrannical king who was forced to abdicate after a poet's satire, then led the Fomorians to war.
Epic Tale card. Kernunos is the personification of nature, forests, and fertility — a mysterious god apart from other gods.
Epic Tale card. Queen Maeve of Connacht shares her wealth with her people and goes to war to capture the Brown Bull of Cooley.
Epic Tale card. The Tuatha De Danann who came from the northern islands, fought against the Fir Bolg and Fomorians, and later withdrew to the Otherworld.
Epic Tale card. A band of mythical warriors led by Fionn mac Cumhaill, one of the great Celtic heroes — warrior, poet, and magician.
Epic Tale card. King of the Tuatha De Danann who abdicated after losing his arm but regained the throne when Dian Cecht made him a silver prosthetic arm.
Epic Tale card. Lug, master of all arts and sciences, led the Tuatha De Danann during the Second Battle of Moytura after Nuada offered him command.
Epic Tale card. The kind god and protector of tribes — a druid, warrior, sage, harbinger of life and death, and master of time and seasons.
Be present in 6 or more territories. Each territory where you have at least 1 clan counts. Deed tokens can supplement this count.
Be chieftain over a combined total of 6 or more opposing clans. Add up all opposing clans in territories where you are chieftain. Deed tokens can supplement this count.
Be present in territories with a combined total of 6 or more Sanctuaries. You do not need to have built the Sanctuaries — just be present in those territories. Deed tokens can supplement this count.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Used in the recommended Discovery Scenario as a starting territory for all player counts.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Used in the Discovery Scenario as a starting territory for all player counts.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Used in the Discovery Scenario as a starting territory for 3 and 4 player games.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Used in the Discovery Scenario as a starting territory for 4-player games. Its Advantage card allows a special move action.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Part of the Discovery Scenario bottom stack.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Part of the Discovery Scenario bottom stack.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Part of the Discovery Scenario bottom stack.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Part of the Discovery Scenario bottom stack. Has a special effect related to the Flock of Crows token.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Part of the Discovery Scenario bottom stack.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Part of the Discovery Scenario bottom stack.
One of the 16 territory tiles. Its Advantage card allows moving a clan from the Lost Vale into an adjacent territory without initiating a clash.

The recommended setup for your first game. Uses specific starting territories (Valley, Cove, Plains, Hills based on player count) and stacks certain territory tiles at the bottom of the draw stack to ensure they appear later in the game.

In 2-player games, the Flock of Crows token is not used (except for Gates of Tir Na nOg). The Action card draft uses two separate 3-card drafts instead of the standard 4-card draft, resulting in 6 Action cards per player.
